fixtures.name: blank.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Baseline for measuring heap/resource noise introduced by adding controls, constants, terminals, and wires.

fixtures.name: Numeric42.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: The trivial compressed-wire payload encodes a direct source-to-sink chain when terminal anchors are aligned.

fixtures.name: Numeric4Dot2.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Changing numeric type updates VCTP/type metadata without changing the simple wire topology.

fixtures.name: BoolToLED.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Boolean widgets and terminals use the same compressed-wire chain family as numeric terminals.

fixtures.name: Add17Plus25.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Adding a primitive introduces block-diagram primitive metadata while preserving independently decodable terminal anchors.

fixtures.name: Numeric42Far.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Horizontal sink movement stretches the inferred post-elbow segment rather than changing the source-side anchor.

fixtures.name: Numeric42FarFar.vi
focus: anchorX is not the x distance
fixtures.hypothesis: Sink ~400 px down and > 400 px right emits 04 08 00 00 e2 ff 01 90. yStep = 0xff 01 90 = 0x0190 = exactly 400, confirming the 0xff big-endian escape decodes 1:1 for magnitudes >= 255. anchorX = 0xe2 = 226 is the elbow offset, NOT the >400 px horizontal distance (which lives in the terminal bounds) — an earlier save had anchorX=470 at a SMALLER x offset, so anchorX is an edit-history artifact, not a distance.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_8px_down_auto-connect.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Authored from Numeric42.vi by deleting the wire, moving the indicator 8 px down, and reconnecting — so LabVIEW auto-routes from scratch and places the elbow at the canonical ~16 px (04 08 00 00 10 08, anchorX=16, yStep=8 down). Clean stretch-vs-reconnect counterpart to Numeric42_8px_down.vi (same 8px-down geometry, but stretched to anchorX=65).

fixtures.name: Numeric42Bend.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A one-bend auto route uses a four-varint chain payload with direction, reserved zero, source anchor, and y-step magnitude.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_8px_down.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the sink 8 px down changes only the y-step magnitude in the chain payload.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_16px_down.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Doubling the vertical movement doubles the y-step magnitude.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_150px_down.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the sink 150 px down (past the 127 boundary) encodes the y-step as the single raw byte 0x96=150, not LEB128 (which would need 96 01). Proves auto-chain geometry magnitudes are raw bytes, so ChainAutoPath must drop the {16,65} anchor whitelist and decode values 0-255 verbatim.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_8px_up.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the sink above the source flips a sign/direction field while preserving the y-step magnitude.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_8px_down_8px_further_right.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the sink right after the wire was drawn STRETCHES the post-elbow segment: the wire chunk is byte-identical to Numeric42_8px_down.vi (04 08 00 00 41 08, sourceAnchorX=65 frozen) — horizontal position lives in the terminal bounds, not the chunk, for a rightward route.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_8px_down_8px_further_right_auto_connect.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Same terminal positions as Numeric42_8px_down_8px_further_right.vi but the wire was deleted and reconnected, so the auto-router recomputes the elbow at the canonical short distance: 04 08 00 00 10 08 (sourceAnchorX=16). Proves sourceAnchorX is edit-history-dependent, not a glyph constant — the chunk depends on how the wire was last routed, not just final terminal positions.

fixtures.name: Numeric42TwoNetworks.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Two disconnected const-to-indicator pairs emit two independent compressed-wire chunks.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_indicator_left.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Placing the indicator left of the source forces LabVIEW to auto-route a 6-waypoint (byte0=6) path; observed payload `06 08 00 00 00 00 10 0f 34 23` that ChainAutoPath rejects (>4 varints). A near-aligned variant of the left_auto family (prefix `10 0f`, p1=00) — kept as an independent multi-elbow sample distinct from the calibrated left_auto_* set.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_left_auto_8px_up.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Indicator placed left of and 8px above the source produces a 6-waypoint doubling-back auto route `06 08 00 01 01 00 10 10 9c 18`. Baseline of the calibrated left_auto family: p0/p2 encode vertical direction (up=(00,01)), p6 the horizontal seed, p7=16+pixels the vertical magnitude (here 0x18=24 → 8px).

fixtures.name: Numeric42_left_auto_16px_up.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Same layout as left_auto_8px_up but indicator 16px above source: `06 08 00 01 01 00 10 10 9c 20`. Only p7 changes (0x18→0x20, +8 for +8px), confirming p7 = 16 + vertical pixels at 1 unit/pixel.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_left_auto_8px_down.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Same X as the up fixtures but indicator 8px below source: `06 08 01 01 00 00 10 10 9c 18`. Versus left_auto_8px_up only p0/p2 flip (down=(01,00)) while p6/p7 are unchanged, isolating vertical direction from magnitude and from horizontal position.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_left_auto_8px_down+8px_right.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: left_auto_8px_down with the indicator moved 8px right (closer to the source): `06 08 01 01 00 00 10 10 94 18`. Only p6 changes (0x9c→0x94, −8 for +8px right), confirming p6 = horizontal seed at 1 unit/pixel, decreasing as the sink approaches the source.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_manual_elbows.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Manual elbow edits made in the LabVIEW editor were re-snapped to a single-elbow auto-route by LabVIEW's wire cleanup; observed payload `04 08 00 00 fe 70` is a normal 4-waypoint auto-chain (byte1=0x08, not 0x04). Confirms that manual-mode (byte1=0x04) requires a non-orthogonal drag the editor cannot fold back; Numeric42Bend.vi remains the only manual-chain ground truth.

fixtures.name: ConnectorPane_2Inputs_2Outputs.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A 2-2 connector pane with 4 distinct FP terminals (2 unwired inputs, 2 wired outputs from constants) produces a single root-level SL__sdfTun whose children declare 6 endpoint IDs. The two output wires terminate on distinct conPane endpoints that currently share the sdfTun anchor — needed for §16.4 follow-up: per-endpoint connector-pane geometry.

fixtures.name: WhileLoop_Numeric42.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A while-loop containing a constant-to-indicator wire produces a nested SL__sdfTun [16] inside the loop's wrapper [14] (own ID 165), in addition to the outer root-level SL__sdfTun [3]. Confirms that loops/structures nest WidgetKindTerminal nodes inside other WidgetKindTerminal nodes — needed to validate the §16.4 follow-up: project nested terminals into the scene.

fixtures.name: Numeric42TwoIndicatorsY.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A two-output fan-out uses tree mode with trailing endpoint records.

fixtures.name: Numeric42TwoIndicatorsY_top7right_bottom11down.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving each branch endpoint perturbs the matching endpoint record and leaves structural records stable.

fixtures.name: Numeric42ThreeIndicatorsTfork.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A three-output fan-out with one indicator far to the right (T-fork, not a pure vertical Y-stack) emits byte0=7: the straight-through leg is a genuine far-right (V, H) endpoint ({V:66, H:196}); the two tap records' second byte is a branch offset, not a horizontal coordinate.

fixtures.name: Numeric42ThreeIndicatorsTfork_bottom8pxdown.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the bottom tap 8 px down changes exactly one trailing record by +8, in the byte TreeEndpoints labels H — confirming the byte tracks the edited branch while showing it is a branch offset, not a horizontal coordinate.

fixtures.name: Numeric42ThreeIndicatorsYComb.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Comb topology adds junction/span records that cannot be decoded as a simple trailing endpoint list.

fixtures.name: Numeric42ThreeIndicatorsYComb_middle8pxdown.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Moving the middle branch changes adjacent span/junction records in opposite directions.

fixtures.name: Numeric42FourIndicatorsY_single.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Four outputs can produce a larger topology-dependent tree payload that must remain raw until more comb cases are mapped.

fixtures.name: Numeric255.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: The OF__ConstValue leaf stores an I32 constant as a fixed-width big-endian int32; 255 (0x000000ff) sits at the single-byte boundary without a varint/escape.

fixtures.name: Numeric256.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: 256 (0x00000100) crossing the single-byte boundary keeps the same 4-byte big-endian OF__ConstValue width, proving the literal is positional BE, not a byte-stream-with-escape like the compressed wire geometry.

fixtures.name: Numeric65535.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: 65535 (0x0000ffff) sits at the two-byte boundary; OF__ConstValue stays a fixed 4-byte big-endian int32.

fixtures.name: Numeric65536.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: 65536 (0x00010000) crossing the two-byte boundary still occupies the same 4-byte big-endian OF__ConstValue field, confirming type-width fixed encoding with no escape.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_increment.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A left-to-right primitive (Increment) keeps both terminals side-entered, so each wire stays a 2-corner byte0=04 elbow; an odd-corner byte0=03 route is not reachable from a horizontal-flow node.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_N_terminal.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: Even a top-entered terminal (For-Loop N) keeps a short horizontal stub, so the route stays a 2-corner byte0=04 elbow; byte0=03 is an emergent multi-node artifact, not coercible from a single source-to-sink layout.

fixtures.name: Numeric9876Dot5432.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: OF__ConstValue stores a DBL constant as an 8-byte big-endian IEEE-754 double (9876.5432 = 40 c3 4a 45 87 93 dd 98), confirming the literal width tracks the data type (4 bytes I32, 8 bytes DBL) and is written verbatim with no escape.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_I8.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: An I8 constant's OF__ConstValue is a single raw byte (42 = 0x2a); signedness is carried by the type, not the literal bytes.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_U8.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A U8 constant's OF__ConstValue is a single raw byte identical to the I8 case (42 = 0x2a), proving the literal stores magnitude bytes and leaves signedness to the type metadata.

fixtures.name: Numeric65536_I64.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: An I64 constant's OF__ConstValue is an 8-byte big-endian int64 (65536 = 00 00 00 00 00 01 00 00), confirming integer width tracks the data type.

fixtures.name: Numeric9876Dot5432_SGL.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A SGL constant's OF__ConstValue is a 4-byte big-endian IEEE-754 binary32 (46 1a 52 2c = float32(9876.5432) = 9876.54297), storing the float32 rounding of the typed value.

fixtures.name: Numeric9876Dot5432_EXT.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: An EXT constant's OF__ConstValue is a 16-byte big-endian IEEE-754 binary128/quad (40 0c 34 a4 58 00..., unbiased exponent 13 = 9876.54297), not 80-bit x87; wider than the 8-byte numeric band, so the leaf alone cannot be distinguished from a string without the VCTP type.

fixtures.name: Numeric9876Dot5432_FXD.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A FXD constant's OF__ConstValue is an 8-byte big-endian fixed-point container; 9876.5432 is stored as magnitude 0x26948b = 2528395 = round(9876.5432 * 2^8), with the radix-point position set by the fixed-point word/integer-word-length config.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_CSG.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A CSG (complex single) constant's OF__ConstValue is 8 bytes = two big-endian IEEE-754 binary32 components, real-then-imaginary (42+0i = 42 28 00 00 | 00 00 00 00).

fixtures.name: Numeric42_i5_CDB.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A CDB (complex double) constant's OF__ConstValue is 16 bytes = two big-endian IEEE-754 binary64 components, real-then-imaginary (42+5i = 40 45.. | 40 14..). The 16-byte width is EXT-vs-CDB ambiguous without the VCTP type.

fixtures.name: Numeric42_CEXT.vi
fixtures.hypothesis: A CEXT (complex extended) constant's OF__ConstValue is 32 bytes = two big-endian IEEE-754 binary128 components, real-then-imaginary (42+0i; real half exponent 5, imaginary half all zero).

gaps.notes: Separate label text from label bounds/anchor records.
Distinguish captions from labels and control-owned text fields.
Correlate FTAB entries with heap text/font references.
Map front-panel color fields and panel background storage.
Identify tick/range/format fields for numerics and graphs.
Separate decorative shapes from controls and diagram primitives.
Map array element containers, index displays, and VCTP array descriptors.
Decode graph plot/scale metadata beyond generic widget classification.
Map frame ordering, tunnels, shift registers, and selector metadata.
Expand refnum type descriptors and widget-specific fields.
Ground variant controls and VITS/LVVariant payload interpretation.
Connect BDHb call-site nodes to LIbd/LIvi dependency entries.
Decode event data node, registration, and frame metadata.
Map disabled/conditional diagram structure classes and active-frame fields.
Ground manual-chain compressed-wire varint streams with known waypoints.

The byte0=6 leftward doubling-back route is now decoded and calibrated by the Numeric42_left_auto_* family (Wire.LeftwardChainPath): p0/p2=direction, p6=horizontal seed (1/px), p7=16+vertical pixels. Remaining: an up-direction X pair to confirm p6 behaves the same upward, the absolute p6/p7 zero offsets, and rightward/other multi-elbow shapes (e.g. true 3-elbow routes).
Establish version gates for resource layouts and legacy string/type encodings.

oracleTargets: native LabVIEW screenshot or exported geometry where visual semantics are tested
